title: Cash Dividends — Three Dates
event: Reyes Co. declares a $20,000 cash dividend on March 1 (declaration date), payable April 10 to stockholders of record on March 20. Prepare all required entries.

lines.label: distribution declared
lines.why: Declaration legally commits the company — RE takes the hit now.
lines.trap: Not an expense — dividends are a DISTRIBUTION of income, so they never touch the income statement.

lines.label: owed to holders
lines.why: A genuine current liability from declaration until payment.
lines.trap: The record date (Mar 20) gets NO entry — it only determines who receives the checks.

lines.label: obligation cleared
lines.why: The declared liability is paid off.
lines.trap: RE was already reduced at declaration — touching it again double-counts.

lines.label: checks out
lines.trap: Payment is a pure liability settlement — equity moved back on March 1.

memorize.body: Declare → entry. Record → NO entry. Pay → entry. Two entries, three dates.
Equity falls at DECLARATION, not payment — and dividends require sufficient retained earnings and cash, two separate constraints.

questions.prompt: What entry is recorded on the March 20 DATE OF RECORD?

distractors.feedback: The record date only fixes WHO gets paid — no accounts change. Entries happen at declaration and payment only.

build.scaffold: Two entries bracket the record date. And the decoy: 'Dividend Expense' does not exist.
